name: Devon Days Out — Geofence Configuration
description: Pass-attached locations for Apple Wallet / Google Wallet. When a member's device enters one of these geofences, iOS surfaces the pass on the lock screen with the matching relevantText. Apple permits up to 10 entries per pass.

locations.name: Totnes
locations.relevantText: Welcome to Totnes! Tap to view your local deals.

locations.name: Dartmouth
locations.relevantText: Welcome to Dartmouth! Your harbour is calling.

locations.name: Brixham
locations.relevantText: Welcome to Brixham! 20% off at the Fish Market today.

locations.name: Sidmouth
locations.relevantText: Welcome to Sidmouth! Tap for cream tea offers.

locations.name: Exmouth
locations.relevantText: Welcome to Exmouth! Tap to view your local deals.

locations.name: Brixham Fish Market
locations.relevantText: You're at Brixham Fish Market. 20% off ends 31 May.

locations.name: The Cherub, Dartmouth
locations.relevantText: You're at The Cherub, Dartmouth. Show pass for 10% off.

notes.device_radius: iOS uses ~100m geofence radius by default; Apple controls the precise threshold and battery-aware re-evaluation. The geofence_radius_meters field above is a hint for Google Wallet and PassQR's own renderer.
notes.battery_impact: Geofences are evaluated on-device using significant-location-change events; impact on battery is negligible.
notes.data_privacy: Coordinates leave the device only when the merchant explicitly scans the pass at a venue. Apple does not transmit geofence enter/exit events to PassQR or Visit South Devon.
